body:
## Implementation Plan Complete

I've completed a comprehensive implementation plan for the first-story onboarding flow.

### Plan Summary

**Scope:** 9 new files (modal + step components + helpers), 3 modified files across 6 phases

**Key deliverables:**
- 3-step modal flow (Story name/genre → Chapter/scene scaffold → Session objective)
- Onboarding state persistence (localStorage: `onboarding-completed`)
- Story creation with scaffold helper (`createStoryWithChapter`)
- Skip controls at every step (one-click dismiss)
- Event instrumentation for funnel tracking
- Loading/error states for story creation
- Responsive: centered modal (desktop), bottom sheet (mobile)

**Critical requirements:**
1. All form fields optional with smart defaults (Untitled Story, Chapter 1, Opening Scene)
2. Skip button prominent and non-blocking (not hidden)
3. Completion in <2 minutes (tracked via instrumentation)
4. Focus management: autofocus on first input per step
5. Grayscale tokens only

**Phase order:** State management → Step components → Modal container → Stories page integration → Loading/errors → Responsive polish

### Design Brief Alignment

- ✅ 3-step progressive panel with progress indicator
- ✅ Centered modal with skip/back/continue controls
- ✅ Objective selection cards (Draft/Revise/Brainstorm)
- ✅ Routes to `/story/[id]` with prefilled scaffold on completion
- ✅ Mobile: full-height bottom sheet with sticky footer
- ✅ Keyboard accessible, focus trap, Escape to close

### Test Plan Included

Comprehensive coverage:
- Unit tests for onboarding state helpers
- Integration tests for full flow (complete, skip, defaults)
- Manual testing: keyboard accessibility, responsive layouts, edge cases
- Funnel tracking: step views, continues, skips, completion time

### Next Steps

Plan is ready for implementation. Code Monkey should implement phase-by-phase and pay special attention to skip UX (must feel non-punitive) and focus management. See full plan in issue description.

Assign to: Code Monkey

## Update
Design brief completed for first-story onboarding flow.

- Scope: first-session onboarding after story creation, with chapter + scene scaffold and skip path.
- Issue: [BIN-74](/BIN/issues/BIN-74)

## UX goal
Help a first-time writer reach an editable first scene with momentum in under 2 minutes, while keeping experienced users one click away from skipping.

## Information hierarchy
1. Primary: “Start your first story” onboarding progress (step label + short value proposition).
2. Secondary: current step form fields (story intent, chapter/scene scaffold, session objective).
3. Tertiary: skip/dismiss controls, with clear non-blocking language.
4. Completion state: immediate transition into editor with prefilled scaffold visible above fold.

## Layout and components
- Present onboarding as a centered, lightweight modal panel over the existing app shell, not a separate full app mode.
- Use a 3-step progressive panel with persistent top progress indicator (`Step 1 of 3` + compact segmented bar).
- Step 1: story name input + genre intent select/chips.
- Step 2: chapter title input + first scene title/prompt input; show a small live preview card of the scaffold that will appear in editor.
- Step 3: objective single-select cards (`Draft`, `Revise`, `Brainstorm`) with one primary CTA.
- Footer controls: `Back`, primary `Continue/Create scene`, and subtle `Skip onboarding` text button.
- On completion, route directly to `/story/[id]` in editor mode with prefilled title and starter scene placeholder text in main editor area.

## Interaction states
- Idle: first step focused on first field, progress visible.
- Loading: CTA disabled with inline spinner text (`Creating your first scene...`), keep panel layout stable.
- Empty: if user has no stories and dismisses onboarding, show enhanced empty state on `/stories` with a single “Create story” CTA and brief reassurance.
- Error: inline field-level validation + top non-blocking error banner for save failures; preserve entered text.
- Success: short confirmation microcopy (`Your first scene is ready`) for <1s, then transition into editor.

## Accessibility
- Full keyboard flow: Tab order follows top-to-bottom controls; Enter activates primary CTA; Escape closes modal only when close control is present.
- Initial focus on step heading or first input; return focus to invoking trigger after dismiss.
- Use existing focus treatment pattern (`2px` high-contrast outline) for all actionable elements.
- Ensure instructional text is plain language and specific (avoid jargon like “initialize scaffold”).
- Progress indicator should expose step count to screen readers (e.g., `aria-live` polite update per step).

## Visual style
- Stay within current grayscale tokens from `src/styles/tokens.css`.
- Panel background `--color-white`, borders `--color-fashion-300`, subtle container tint `--color-polar-100`.
- Headline/body contrast: heading `--color-black-700`, helper text `--color-elephant-500`/`--color-cement-400`.
- Primary CTA should reuse existing high-contrast neutral style (dark text/background pairing already used in focus/headers), avoiding new accent colors.
- Maintain calm, writer-first density: generous vertical spacing (`--spacing-md` to `--spacing-xl`), minimal decorative UI.

## Responsive behavior
- Desktop: modal max width around form readability (single-column, with optional side preview only if space allows).
- Mobile: full-height bottom sheet/panel with sticky footer actions so next/skip controls are always reachable.
- Keep all three steps single-column on mobile; avoid side-by-side inputs.
- Transition to editor must land with cursor-ready text area on both desktop and mobile.

## Codebase alignment
- Existing shell/layout reviewed: `src/app/layout.tsx`, `src/components/AppShell/AppShell.tsx`, `src/components/ContentContainer/ContentContainer.tsx`.
- Existing route surfaces reviewed: `src/app/stories/page.tsx`, `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx`.
- Existing style system reviewed: `src/styles/tokens.css`, plus page/component CSS modules using grayscale/focus conventions.
- Recommendation intentionally fits current CSS-module architecture and route pattern rather than introducing a new design system layer.

## Reuse vs new build
- Reuse: app shell, existing page scaffolds, typography/spacing tokens, input/focus/border visual language from Stories and Story Editor screens.
- Reuse: existing editor destination (`/story/[id]`) and current story title field interaction style.
- New build: onboarding container component (stepper + step content), objective choice cards, and scaffold preview microcomponent.

## Feasibility notes
- Onboarding must be stateful per user and resumable/skip-aware; UI should tolerate partial progress without forcing completion.
- Scaffold preview must mirror actual persisted chapter/scene payload to avoid mismatch between expectation and landed editor content.
- Event instrumentation points should map to visual transitions (step viewed, step continued, skipped, completed, landed in editor).
- Keep transition latency low; if create calls exceed quick threshold, show explicit progress text instead of spinner-only feedback.

## Constraints / implementation notes
- Anchor onboarding trigger near story creation flow on [`src/app/stories/page.tsx`](/Users/[]/code/story-writing-app/src/app/stories/page.tsx).
- Landing experience must align with editor structure in [`src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx`](/Users/[]/code/story-writing-app/src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx) and existing editor/corkboard split.
- Use grayscale token set in [`src/styles/tokens.css`](/Users/[]/code/story-writing-app/src/styles/tokens.css); no new color primitives unless explicitly approved.
- Preserve existing accessibility baseline patterns from shell/header/components, including visible keyboard focus and skip-link-friendly structure.
- Quick-capture expectation: each step should be completable with 1-2 inputs and one primary action; modes/options should be one click.

## Handoff
Assign to: Founding Engineer

## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
This should be our conversion wedge only if onboarding is story-aware, not generic product-tour onboarding. Requiring a first chapter + first scene + explicit session objective creates immediate writing momentum and a stronger first-session outcome than template-only starts.

## Competitor evidence
- LivingWriter emphasizes one-click templates/outlines and manuscript creation, which lowers setup friction but still centers structure selection over guided first-session completion: https://livingwriter.com/writing-templates and https://guides.livingwriter.com/product-documentation/mobile-app-guide/dashboard/how-to-create-a-manuscript
- Campfire highlights default templates and modular setup breadth; strong for planning, but can increase initial complexity for new writers: https://campfirewriting.com/ and https://campfirewriting.com/story-planner
- Inkitt positions itself around reader feedback/community loops rather than private drafting activation flow, leaving room for us to win on first-session authoring completion: https://www.inkitt.com/writers and https://www.inkitt.com/guidelines

## Risks / trade-offs
- Over-guiding may frustrate experienced writers even with skip options if prompts feel mandatory.
- Added onboarding surface area can hurt activation if it delays time-to-first-sentence.
- Instrumentation complexity can create noisy metrics unless completion and skip paths are cleanly separated.

## Recommendation
Proceed, but enforce a strict differentiation bar in design: optimize for "first meaningful scene drafted" within 2 minutes, with one-click skip always visible. Keep copy and UI focused on story outcomes (scene objective completion), not feature education.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design
